name: Kinmen County (Taiwan)
description: A green field charged at the centre with the Kinmen County emblem.
history: Kinmen (also spelt Quemoy) is a county comprising several islands just off the coast of mainland China, governed by the Republic of China since 1945 and a major front line during the Cold War. The current county flag and emblem date from the late 20th century, after Kinmen was demilitarised and opened to civilian travel in 1992. The motif combines the literal 'Golden Gate' meaning of the name with symbols of defence and seafaring heritage.

features.symbolism: stylised characters 金門 ('Golden Gate'), with motifs evoking the islands' historical role as a coastal fortress and their maritime setting
